steps.title: Compare familiar and unfamiliar speech
steps.content: The conclusion says younger dogs may be worse at differentiating between familiar and unfamiliar languages. A supporting finding must show that as dog age decreases, the brain-activity difference between familiar and unfamiliar language also decreases.

steps.title: Reject wrong comparisons
steps.content: Choice A shows dogs of all ages distinguished the languages but not that younger dogs did so worse. Choice C concerns overall activity levels, and Choice D compares scrambled recordings with unfamiliar language. The remaining finding shows shrinking language-specific differences in younger dogs. The answer is B.
